indicators.name: island counter
indicators.rationale: The island counter is too low for comfortable keyboarding and mouse use, requiring the user to sit on the floor or use a stool, which is not ergonomic.

indicators.name: low ceiling height
indicators.rationale: The low ceiling height may limit the ability to position a monitor at a comfortable viewing distance, potentially causing neck strain.

indicators.name: no flat personal work surface
indicators.rationale: There is no flat surface at sitting height to place a laptop or keyboard, making it difficult to set up a computer workstation.

indicators.name: no ergonomic seating
indicators.rationale: There is no ergonomic seating available, which is crucial for maintaining correct posture during extended computer use.

indicators.name: no dedicated lighting
indicators.rationale: There is no dedicated task lighting, which can cause screen glare and reduce visibility.

reasoning_summary: This indoor scene is marginally adequate for computer work (solo). The low island counter and lack of ergonomic seating make it difficult to set up a comfortable workstation. Additionally, the low ceiling height and absence of dedicated lighting may pose challenges for extended computer use.
